summary: Governed embedding record for the enhanced skills zip that deepened canonical operational, coordination, and language skill surfaces without creating a parallel runtime package. The March 2026 follow-on import also promotes a distinct MADR skill for file-oriented ADR repository workflows.
validationScope: The imported pack reported passing skill-depth and placeholder validation on the upgraded repo copy. Full release smoke remained host-repo dependent because the export environment still had unrelated upstream gaps.
researchScope: The imported guidance drew on official docs and upstream repositories for ADRs, API governance, database migrations, worktrees, modernization, .NET, TypeScript, JavaScript, Lua, and repo-onboarding workflows. A follow-on user-authored skill added MADR repository conventions, ADR validation, and ADR log generation.

entries.decisionReason: The pack adds richer onboarding triggers, ownership heuristics, and command discovery without changing the canonical responsibility.
The new checklist, archetype, and health-signal references deepen the existing onboarding surface.
Wrapper alignment is planned against the upgraded canonical skill and tracked here so discovery drift stays explicit.
The pack keeps the same ADR responsibility while adding stronger decision framing, supersession, and anti-pattern guidance.
The imported rubric, MADR guidance, and examples materially deepen the canonical ADR surface.
The wrapper remains the discovery entrypoint and will be aligned in the follow-on discovery phase.
The canonical API-review skill already existed; the import upgrades it with protocol-aware review depth and consumer-safety outputs.
The imported checklists, compatibility notes, and review template are runtime-facing guidance for the existing skill.
The imported wrapper should become the project discovery surface, but wrapper rollout is tracked separately from canonical MVP embedding.
The existing migration-review skill keeps ownership while absorbing expand-and-contract, engine-hotspot, and rollout guidance.
The imported risk taxonomy, rollout notes, and review template are first-class supporting references for the canonical skill.
The wrapper is reserved for the discovery-alignment phase so the new workload skill can be surfaced cleanly to agents.
Harness Forge already owned the skill identity, and the import turns the placeholder into a production-grade supervision contract.
The task sharding, stacked-diff, merge-readiness, and worktree-operation references are runtime-facing supervision knowledge.
A new wrapper is appropriate, but the wrapper rollout is tracked separately from the canonical skill merge.
The imported roadmap and seam-first modernization guidance enrich the existing project-owned skill.
The imported modernization layers, safety rails, automation candidates, and roadmap template are reusable runtime-facing references.
The import introduces a missing discovery wrapper and keeps the future agent entrypoint explicit in the inventory.
The seeded TypeScript skill keeps ownership while absorbing stronger activation, validation, and workspace-boundary guidance.
The import adds runtime-validation and monorepo references to the existing engineering pack.
The wrapper remains the auto-discovery entrypoint and will be aligned to the upgraded language contract later in the feature.
The seeded .NET skill already existed and now absorbs stronger solution-graph, DI, migration, and hosting guidance.
The import adds cloud-native and Aspire coverage plus richer supporting heuristics to the current reference pack.
The wrapper stays as the entrypoint and is tracked for later alignment with the deeper canonical skill.
The seeded Lua skill keeps ownership while the import adds runtime-flavor, editor, OpenResty, and tooling guidance.
The imported runtime profiles, editor patterns, OpenResty notes, and tooling guidance deepen the existing language pack.
The wrapper remains a discovery surface and is tracked for later alignment to the upgraded skill contract.
JavaScript engineering is a distinct runtime-focused responsibility that already exists in Harness Forge and is now promoted with deeper contracts and packaging guidance.
The imported browser, bundler, and package-contract references deepen the promoted JavaScript skill.
The wrapper exists today and will be aligned in the discovery phase so agents see the richer JS contract.
The pack README documents the source bundle contents and intent but should not ship as a second front door.
Pack-local validation context matters for maintainers, but the project keeps release truth in its own validation surfaces.
The research summary informs maintainers and future imports, but runtime agents should rely on the canonical project-owned skills instead.
The imported MADR skill owns a distinct file-oriented ADR repository workflow rather than duplicating the broader architecture-decision-records responsibility.
The reference pack supplies the canonical MADR template, conventions, and review checklist needed by the new skill.
The helper scripts make ADR scaffolding, validation, and ADR-LOG generation deterministic and reusable.
The wrapper keeps the MADR workflow discoverable while the canonical execution contract remains under skills/.

embeddingRules.matchCriteria: An imported skill has the same responsibility as an existing canonical Harness Forge skill.
requiredFollowUps: record the decision in the inventory
validate section depth and reference paths
exceptions: Promote instead when the imported responsibility is distinct enough to deserve a first-class skill identity.

embeddingRules.matchCriteria: An imported runtime-facing skill fills a distinct responsibility that should be discoverable in the packaged skill catalog.
requiredFollowUps: create or update the canonical skill directory
reflect the skill in catalogs and discovery docs
exceptions: Do not promote when the content is just a narrower variant of an existing project-owned responsibility.

embeddingRules.matchCriteria: An imported wrapper changes or introduces an auto-discovery surface under .agents/skills/.
requiredFollowUps: record the wrapper destination in the inventory
align wrapper wording to the canonical skill
validate that the wrapper points at the active canonical surface
exceptions: Wrapper execution may be staged after canonical skill embedding, but pending rollout must stay explicit.

embeddingRules.matchCriteria: Imported materials describe pack intent, research, or validation rather than runtime guidance.
requiredFollowUps: summarize the material in maintainer documentation
keep runtime-facing skills separate from provenance
reference the inventory rather than shipping a parallel package root
exceptions: Runtime guidance that directly improves an active canonical skill belongs in skills/ or references/ instead.
